L1.area: Collection element ownership transfer and iteration utilities

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in collection element move operations where elements read via raw pointer duplication are not properly guarded against panic unwinding. If a user-provided closure panics during element consumption, previously moved elements may be dropped twice, leading to undefined behavior including potential memory corruption.

L3.area: Collection element ownership transfer and iteration utilities
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in collection element move operations where elements read via raw pointer duplication are not properly guarded against panic unwinding. If a user-provided closure panics during element consumption, previously moved elements may be dropped twice, leading to undefined behavior including potential memory corruption.

ground_truth.reason: The SliceMoveSource and SliceMoveSourceCollection trait implementations for arrays and boxed slices use ptr::read to duplicate element ownership without inhibiting drops on the source, causing a double free if the user-provided closure panics during unwinding.
